sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial T1 C+ FS MR in the same patient shows the outer membrane is thick and enhances uniformly. The sSDH is slightly hyperintense compared to sulcal CSF.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: RP-09

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial graphic shows a typical subacute subdural hematoma (sSDH) with local mass effect. Inset shows the traversing "bridging" vein and developing membranes . These are often related to relatively minor trauma in older adults. sSDHs are ~ 3 days to 3 weeks in age.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-01

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: SDHs decrease by ~ 1.5 HU/day. By 7-10 days, blood in the hematoma becomes isodense with the cortex. By ~ 10-14 days, it is hypodense compared to the adjacent brain.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-02

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial NECT in this patient with subacute right SDH , which can be difficult to delineate, as it is almost exactly the same attenuation as the underlying gray matter, is shown. The displaced gray-white matter interface toward midline helps make the accurate diagnosis.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-03

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Coronal NECT in an older adult with multiple falls shows a subacute right SDH that is isodense with the underlying cortex. Note the inward displacement of CSF and compressed, underlying sulci.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-04

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial NECT scan in an older adult with a late sSDH and moderate cortical atrophy shows the difference between the nearly isodense SDH and the hypodense CSF in the underlying compressed subarachnoid spaces .
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-05

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial T1 MR in a 61-year-old man shows a late sSDH that appears nearly isointense with the cortex and only slightly more hyperintense than the adjacent sulcal CSF. Note the thin hyperintense membrane overlying the sSDH.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-06

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in the same patient shows the sSDH as uniformly hyperintense . Note mild compression and effacement of the underlying sulci compared to the normal left hemispheric sulci.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-07

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Coronal T1 C+ MR obtained slightly later in the same patient shows the outer membrane surrounding the sSDH is thicker than the thin inner membrane . The sSDH is slightly hyperintense compared to sulcal CSF. DWI may be helpful to exclude an empyema.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-08

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Coronal NECT in a patient without a history of trauma demonstrates almost perfectly isodense bilateral SDHs . Look for displaced cortical veins and gray-white matter interface to aid in diagnosis.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-09

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Sagittal NECT in the same patient shows findings of intracranial hypotension as the etiology for the bilateral sSDH. There is effacement of the suprasellar cistern, midbrain slumping with a decreased distance, and a "fat" pituitary gland .
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-10

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial NECT scan in a 59-year-old man with seizures and no definite history of head trauma shows a right frontal extraaxial fluid collection that is slightly hyperdense relative to CSF in the underlying sulci .
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-11

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: T1 MR in the same patient shows there are bilateral subdural collections that appear slightly hyperintense compared with CSF. This is a typical MR finding in sSDHs.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-12

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial FLAIR scan in the same patient shows that the subacute fluid collections do not suppress, illustrating their difference compared with normal CSF.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-13

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: T2 MR in the same patient shows that both collections are isointense with CSF in the underlying subarachnoid cisterns.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-14

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial T1 C+ MR in the same patient shows that the outer membrane of the sSDH enhances . These findings are consistent with late sSDHs.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-15

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial T2 MR demonstrates a fluid level within this early sSDH , largely hypointense in signal characteristics. FLAIR MR is generally the most sensitive sequence to identify a small SDH.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-16

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial DWI MR shows mixed signal within the sSDH with a hypointense rim . Signal characteristics of blood are not easily predictable on DWI. Susceptibility is often present, but the significance is uncertain. If a hyperintense outer rim is seen on DWI, it is correlated to "unstable" membranes and predictive of future SDH growth.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-17

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: An infant with suspected nonaccidental trauma shows bilateral SDH , status post recent left-sided burr hole with air. Note that vessels in the subarachnoid space are displaced inward due to mass effect from this sSDH. This SDH is better evaluated on subsequent MR.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-18

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial NECT shows a mixed-density sSDH . The more linear areas of high attenuation suggest internal membranes with recent hemorrhage into a preexisting collection.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-19

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Coronal FLAIR MR in an 89-year-old man imaged for cognitive decline shows bilateral hypointense subdurals . Septations seen here suggest a nonacute nature with the propensity for recurrent bleeding. Note that FLAIR is inaccurate for staging chronicity of hematomas.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-20

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Coronal T1 MR shows bilateral SDHs of differing ages: An older right hematoma with methemoglobin and an acute-early subacute left SDH .
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-21

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Sagittal T1 MR shows a hyperintense extraaxial collection compressing adjacent brain parenchyma, consistent with subacute blood (methemoglobin).
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-22

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial T1 MR in the same patient shows the hyperintense left-sided sSDH . Smaller, more recent isointense SDHs are seen over the right hemisphere.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-23

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Axial NECT shows a right sSDH that is isodense with the underlying cortex. Note the inward displacement of "dots" of CSF in compressed, underlying sulci. The gray matter-white matter interface is "buckled" inward (compared with normal-appearing left side).
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-24

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: Bilateral "balanced" isodense sSDHs are present in the case. Note that both gray matter-white matter interfaces are inwardly displaced, and the underlying sulci are compressed. A displaced "dot" of CSF is seen under the left sSDH.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-25

sourceQualifiedImages.caption: SDHs of different ages are common in older anticoagulated patients. The right SDH is subacute (isodense with underlying brain), and the left-sided collection is more chronic-appearing. Note the severe compression of the underlying brain and sulci. The falx is partially calcified.
sourceQualifiedImages.label: SDX-26

downloadSentinel:
[Download the TSV](sandbox:/mnt/data/radprimer_cards.tsv)

CORE_EVIDENCE_FILE_BEGIN
CORE_EVIDENCE_STATUS: USED
CORE_SOURCE_BASIS: Core Radiology PDF uploaded in current environment; Neuro Brain, Cerebral trauma / Extra-axial hemorrhage / Subdural hematoma; page label Neuro Brain: 701.
CORE_FACTS_USED:

* SDH is crescentic extraaxial blood beneath dura.
* SDH can cross cranial sutures.
* Subacute isodense SDH occurs approximately 1–3 weeks after injury.
* Key clues: mass effect, white matter buckling, apparently thickened cortex.
CORE_DERIVED_CARDS:
* Definition, UNKNOWN reasoning, mechanisms, boards traps, and high-yield CT recognition cards.
CORE_LIMITATIONS:
* Core page number from PDF text label; exact PDF page index not exported into TSV.
CORE_EVIDENCE_FILE_END

RADPRIMER_CARD_TSV_DOWNLOAD_READY
FILENAME: radprimer_cards.tsv
EXPECTED_ROWS: 51
